title: Fix global supply-shares lineage — wire fact_GlobalSupplyShares CSV into the pipeline (replaces 22x-duplicated bronze)
description: bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (what silver -> fact_supply_share is built on) holds 56,342 rows = exactly 22x its real source. Confirmed in oem_lh: total 56,342 / distinct 2,561, and distinct (2,561) matches the source CSV fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv exactly. The current table is a one-off bad load (NOT produced by the pipeline — the pipeline only ingests the EU CSV). Fix: add a copy activity that ingests the Global CSV from the source GitHub repo into bronze_GlobalSupplyShares with OverwriteSchema, so the pipeline produces the correct 2,561-row table and keeps it fresh. Discovered via FR-004 (audit C-02 -> /iterate grounding -> Fabric verification -> source-repo trace).
acceptanceCriteria: A copy activity `bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares` is added to pipeline-content.json, cloned from `bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares` (DelimitedTextSource over HttpServerLocation, GET), with sink table `bronze_GlobalSupplyShares` and `tableActionOption: OverwriteSchema` (so a run replaces the 22x-duplicated table with clean data).

[Fabric/Erik] The activity's HTTP source resolves to `Data/fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv` in the source repo (erikemilsson/EU-Critical-Raw-Materials-CRM-Reports-Datasets) — i.e. the connection/relativeUrl points at the GLOBAL file, not the EU one.
After a pipeline run, `bronze_GlobalSupplyShares` has 2,561 rows with distinct == total (no duplication): verify `df.count() == df.dropDuplicates().count() == 2561`.

EU-vs-Global decision recorded: keep `bronze_EUSupplyShares` / `bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares` as a secondary EU cut, OR retire it (silver reads Global; the EU table is currently orphaned — read by nothing).

Downstream re-verified on the corrected data: silver_* + `fact_supply_share` rebuilt from 2,561 rows; check whether the gold/report supply-shares numbers change materially (they were computed on 22x-inflated data — sums/counts were inflated; averages were not).
FR-004 resolved on completion; spec lineage reconciled via /iterate (document the corrected pipeline -> bronze_GlobalSupplyShares -> silver flow + the EU/Global relationship).
notes: ROOT CAUSE: bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (56,342 rows, lastModified 2025-08-25) is NOT written by the orchestrated pipeline — it was a one-off/manual load that duplicated each of the 2,561 source rows 22x. The pipeline's only supply-shares activity (`bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares`) ingests the EU CSV -> bronze_EUSupplyShares (907 rows = its CSV, correct) and is orphaned (nothing reads EU). Because the project scope is GLOBAL (broader than the EU-focused source report), silver correctly wants the Global table — it just was never wired into the pipeline.

SOURCE: https://github.com/erikemilsson/EU-Critical-Raw-Materials-CRM-Reports-Datasets , file `Data/fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv` (2,561 rows; cols Material,Stage,Country,Share,t where t = trade-related variable per the repo's data_cleaning.md; 2023 EU CRM report Appendix 7). Raw URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erikemilsson/EU-Critical-Raw-Materials-CRM-Reports-Datasets/main/Data/fact_GlobalSupplyShares%26t.csv (note %26 encodes the & in the filename).

WHY OverwriteSchema fixes it: a fresh copy with OverwriteSchema replaces the whole table, so the 22x duplication disappears the first time the new activity runs. The bad load was a one-off and won't recur once the pipeline owns the table.

WHY P1: this is a data-correctness bug in the core medallion pipeline — the global supply-shares facts in the gold layer / Power BI report are built on 22x-inflated bronze data. Correctness outranks the portfolio-polish work (021 was P2, 020 P3).

DON'T deeply chase the origin of the 22x (it was a one-off manual load outside the repo); the fix supersedes it. If curious, a GROUP BY on (Material,Stage,Country,Share,t) HAVING COUNT(*)>1 will show the uniform 22x repetition.
--- HANDOFF ---

CLAUDE: write the `bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares` activity JSON in pipeline-content.json (clone the EU activity; change name + sink table; keep DelimitedText/HTTP/GET shape). NOTE the source file path / connection is Fabric-side — flag what Erik must set (the relativeUrl/connection for the Global CSV) since the EU activity's file path lives in the Fabric connection, not the pipeline JSON.

ERIK (Fabric UI): wire the copy activity's HTTP source to the Global CSV (new connection or relativeUrl = fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv), deploy, run the pipeline, confirm bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 2,561 distinct rows, then re-run bronze->silver->gold and eyeball the supply-shares numbers. Decide keep-or-retire the EU table.
AUDIT-DERIVED: not spec-decomposed (empty drift fingerprints). Tracked via FR-004.

DATA SEMANTICS (analyzed both source CSVs 2026-06-22): EU table (907) = EU import/SOURCING shares per material per source-country (rounded to whole %, >=1% only, sums ~94% per material+stage because sub-1% sources are dropped). Global table (2561) = global PRODUCTION/supply shares per material per country (precise decimals incl <0.1%, sums ~100%). Same measure NAME, different meaning. 71 materials shared; 115 of EU's 121 countries are in Global (the 6 'EU-only' are spelling variants: Brasil/South Korea/Surinam/Libanon/Grenada/Mauritius). Global adds 48 countries + 14 materials. Example: Aluminium extraction from Brazil = EU-import 12% vs global-production 10.4% (different numbers for the same material+country). => Global is NOT 'the countries outside the EU' — it's the worldwide superset that covers EU's source countries + more.

DECISION (EU keep/retire): KEEP the EU table — it is analytically central (it IS the 'shares imported to the EU from those countries' the analysis should start from), not a throwaway subset. So wire BOTH CSVs: EU already correct (907); fix Global dup.

MODELING QUESTION (newly surfaced, bigger than the dup fix): silver/fact_supply_share currently read ONLY bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (global production). The EU table (which matches the stated EU-sourcing-risk goal) is ORPHANED — read by nothing. Decide whether the gold supply-shares facts should be driven by EU import shares, global production shares, or BOTH (EU exposure x global concentration is a standard supply-risk lens). This is a gold-model decision for Erik; may warrant its own task/decision record.

METHODOLOGY CONFIRMATION (EU CRM Guidelines, EC 2017, doi:10.2873/769526, read 2026-06-22): interpretation confirmed + modeling question ANSWERED -> BOTH tables required. Official Supply Risk formula: SR = [HHI(GS)*IR/2 + HHI(EUsourcing)*(1-IR/2)] * (1-EoL_RIR) * SI_SR, where GS = global supply (the Global table) and EUsourcing = actual sourcing to the EU = EU domestic production + imports (the EU table). Both are inputs, weighted by import reliance IR (IR=100% -> 50/50 global/EU; IR=0 -> 100% EU sourcing). HHI_WGI,t = sum_c (S_c)^2 * WGI_c * t_c, so per-country share is squared, weighted by governance (WGI = the project's silver_WB / World Bank table) and the trade parameter t. The CSV 't' column IS the trade parameter t_c: 0.8 for EU countries, 1.0 non-EU baseline, >1 for export-restricted (matches observed t distribution; EU table has proportionally more 0.8s because EU sourcing includes more EU suppliers). Stage column E/P = the extraction/processing 'bottleneck' stage (SR taken at the higher-risk stage). => the gold supply-risk model should COMBINE both tables per the SR formula; it ALSO needs import reliance per material ((Import-Export)/(Domestic+Import-Export), Eurostat -- check if present in project), recycling rate EoL_RIR, and substitution index SI_SR. Currently silver reads ONLY Global -> any SR computed is missing the EU-sourcing half.

CROSS-REF: SR gold-model fidelity decision = DEC-001 (decision-001-sr-gold-model.md). task-022 (dup fix) is a PREREQUISITE for it, NOT blocked by it — fix the duplication regardless of which SR model is chosen. The future 'build SR gold model' task will carry decision_dependencies:[DEC-001].

CLAUDE HALF DONE 2026-06-22: bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares activity written into pipeline-content.json (clone of the EU copy activity; sink=bronze_GlobalSupplyShares, OverwriteSchema, 5-col String mapping Material/Stage/Country/Share/t; added to the 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' dependsOn so cleaning waits for it). JSON validated. REMAINING (Erik/Fabric): (1) repoint the new activity's SOURCE connection to the Global CSV — it currently inherits the EU connection GUID 97124587 (would load EU 907 rows into bronze_GlobalSupplyShares); use a connection for https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erikemilsson/EU-Critical-Raw-Materials-CRM-Reports-Datasets/main/Data/fact_GlobalSupplyShares%26t.csv ; (2) deploy the pipeline change to Fabric; (3) run; (4) verify bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 2561 rows, distinct==total.

2026-07-19 (/health-check): confirmed In Progress is accurate — Claude-half drafted and verified, awaiting Erik's Fabric session as #4 in the P1 apply order. Age reflects waiting on Fabric, not stalled work. NOW ALSO A DEC-001 PREREQUISITE: Option B (dual governance/trade-weighted HHI) computes on supply shares, so the 22x duplication must be gone before any HHI is meaningful. See task-038.

VERIFIED 2026-07-20 (/work, verify-agent, attempt 1 = pass). Had sat In Progress with verification_attempts:0 and no user_review_pending since 2026-06-22 — invisible to the waiting-on-you queue and to verification-debt routing (which only inspects Finished tasks), unlike siblings 023/025/026 verified 2026-07-14. Status kept 'In Progress' (NOT 'Finished') to match those siblings: work-procedures.md maps pass -> Finished, but for owner:'both' tasks whose acceptance is majority-Fabric that would overstate completion. Divergence deliberate and surfaced. Remaining: acceptance #2/#3/#5 via the new test_protocol.

EVIDENCE 2026-07-22 19:11 (Erik, Fabric notebook): crit 3 PASSES — spark.table('bronze_GlobalSupplyShares') returns 2561 / 2561 distinct. Crit 2 is proven by implication: only the Global CSV has 2561 rows (the EU file has 907), so the copy activity is resolving the correct source after a3f5aaf repointed it to connection 172b089e. Crit 1 was verified 2026-07-20. Pipeline run 6:41 PM: all 5 bronze activities + bronze-to-silver + silver-to-gold Succeeded, so silver_* and fact_supply_share are rebuilt on the clean 2561-row source (crit 5, first half).

REMAINING for task-022 (3 of 6 criteria open): crit 4 — the EU keep-or-retire DECISION is still unrecorded (bronze_EUSupplyShares is read by nothing; bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares still runs every pipeline). This is Erik's call, not a Claude action. crit 5 second half — compare gold/report supply-shares numbers against the pre-fix values and confirm the change is explained (sums/counts were inflated by the 22x duplication; averages were not). crit 6 — resolve FR-004 and reconcile spec lineage via /iterate.

CROSS-REF task-026 FINDING 2: the 2561/2561 result proves the bronze source carries NO duplicate rows, so the grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share failure is manufactured downstream (UNKNOWN-key collapse at silver-to-gold2:1437-1444 + constant year literal at :1119), not inherited from this source. Useful when working crit 5 — a material change in the gold numbers may partly reflect newly-unmapped Global countries/materials collapsing into UNKNOWN, which is a task-023/025 alias-coverage question.

CRIT 4 RESOLVED 2026-07-22 (Erik): KEEP bronze_EUSupplyShares and bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares. Rationale: the table is orphaned TODAY (engine-consumer audit found only two references — the copy activity that populates it in pipeline-content.json, and an entry in the semantic model's `annotation PBI_RemovedChildren` in expressions.tmdl, which is a record of DELIBERATE EXCLUSION from the DirectLake model, not a consumer. No notebook reads it; there is no silver_eusupplyshares; fact_supply_share is built from silver_globalsupplyshares). BUT it is load-bearing for ratified future work: DEC-001 Option B selected the dual global/EU-sourcing HHI, and task-038 acceptance criterion 3 requires 'The EU sourcing table is read into silver and modeled alongside the global supply table (task-022 supplies the corrected bronze lineage)'. Retiring it would force task-038 to re-establish the same copy activity and HttpServer connection 97124587. Cost of keeping is one ~26s copy activity per pipeline run. Decision: keep; revisit only if DEC-001 is superseded.

CRIT 5 METHOD DECIDED 2026-07-22 (Erik accepted the recommendation): do NOT attempt a literal before/after comparison — no baseline exists (the pre-fix data was overwritten and the old numbers survive only as the qualitative description '22x-inflated', never as recorded values). Substitute an INTERNAL-CONSISTENCY check against a known-correct invariant: supply shares are percentages (the DQ framework bounds share_pct to 0-100), so SUM(share_pct) per (material_key, stage_key) must be ~100. On the old 22x-duplicated data the same query would have returned ~2200. This yields the before/after signal without a baseline because the broken state's expected output is known a priori. Note it isolates cleanly from the mapping problem: UNKNOWN-key collapse preserves share_pct values, so query 1 passes or fails on inflation alone, independent of Finding 2.

CRIT 5 RESULTS 2026-07-22 (Erik, notebook_throwaway). Reconciliation: bronze_GlobalSupplyShares 2561 -> silver_globalsupplyshares 2561 -> fact_supply_share 2561. Perfect pass-through, zero rows dropped (no invalid-stage drops occurred). Mapping: 0 unmapped materials, 47 unmapped countries out of 2561 (98.2% country coverage). Duplicate grains: 2. Share sums per (material, stage): 46 groups fall outside 100% +/- 1, spanning 88.7% to 119.5%.

CRIT 5 VERDICT — the inflation question PASSES. On the old 22x-duplicated data these group sums would have returned ~2200%; they return ~100%. That is the criterion's headline question ('did the numbers change materially, and in the direction the 22x duplication predicts') answered affirmatively, and it is corroborated by the clean 2561->2561->2561 reconciliation. Recommend crit 5 be accepted as met.

NEW OBSERVATION (not part of crit 5, needs its own decision): share_pct does not sum to 100% per (material, stage) — 46 of roughly 74 possible material x stage groups are outside +/-1%, ranging 88.7% to 119.5%, with values both above and below 100. This is NOT explained by the 22x duplication (gone), the UNKNOWN collapse (share values are preserved through it, so group sums are unaffected), or the 2 duplicate grains (negligible across 46 groups). Most likely the source CSV is only approximately normalized — real EU CRM supply-share data aggregates multiple reporting sources with rounding and occasionally overlapping country categories. Needs source-data characterization before any gold-layer normalization is considered, because normalizing to 100% would silently discard whatever the >100% excess represents. Candidate home: task-038 (the HHI work depends directly on share semantics — HHI over shares that sum to 119.5% is not comparable to HHI over shares summing to 93%).

issues.description: New activity inherits EU connection GUID 97124587: an unread deploy would silently load 907 EU sourcing rows into bronze_GlobalSupplyShares under OverwriteSchema — passing a distinct==total dedup check AND the DQ row-count band (100,100000) — rather than failing loudly. Recommend an unresolvable placeholder or a post-run row-count assertion pinned to 2561.
Acceptance #6 half-satisfied: spec § Data Architecture #4 documents the EU/Global relationship, but § Orchestration still lists 4 Stage-1 activities where the pipeline now has 5 (FR-008).
FR-004 remains status 'open' in friction.jsonl; acceptance #6 requires resolution on completion, pending Erik's Fabric run.

Pre-existing § Orchestration drift not attributable to task-022: documents a Stage-4 'Copy job1' InvokeCopyJob absent from the pipeline, and 'Retry: 0 attempts' where all activities carry retry:3.

test_protocol.summary: Erik's Fabric half — repoint the Global source connection, deploy, run, and confirm the 22x duplication is gone (acceptance #2, #3, #5).
test_protocol.automated_results: Local structural verification passed: activity is an exact 60-leaf clone of the EU activity differing only in name, description, and sink table; OverwriteSchema and the GET/HttpServerLocation shape confirmed; dependsOn wiring into the silver notebook confirmed; silver consumer confirmed to read bronze_GlobalSupplyShares. Acceptance #2/#3/#5 cannot be verified without a live Fabric workspace.

steps.instruction: In the Fabric UI, open bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares and repoint its HTTP source connection to the Global CSV: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erikemilsson/EU-Critical-Raw-Materials-CRM-Reports-Datasets/main/Data/fact_GlobalSupplyShares%26t.csv (%26 encodes the &). DO THIS BEFORE ANY RUN — the activity currently inherits the EU connection 97124587.
steps.expected: The activity's source connection is distinct from bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares' connection and resolves to the fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv file.

steps.instruction: Deploy the pipeline change to the Fabric workspace and run the pipeline.
steps.expected: All 5 Stage-1 bronze activities succeed; 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' starts only after all 5 complete.

steps.instruction: In a notebook against oem_lh, run: df = spark.table('bronze_GlobalSupplyShares'); print(df.count(), df.dropDuplicates().count())
steps.expected: 2561 2561 — total equals distinct, confirming the 22x duplication (56,342) is gone. If you see 907, the connection was not repointed and EU data was loaded instead.

steps.instruction: Confirm bronze_EUSupplyShares is unchanged and still loading independently.
steps.expected: 907 rows, sourced from its own connection — the two activities must not share a source.

steps.instruction: Re-run bronze->silver->gold and compare the supply-shares figures in the report against the previous run.
steps.expected: Sums and counts drop by roughly 22x; averages/shares are materially unchanged. Investigate any average that shifts significantly.

steps.instruction: Decide keep-or-retire for the EU table and confirm it matches the recorded decision.
steps.expected: KEEP — already recorded in task notes and spec § Data Architecture #4 (both tables required by the DEC-001 Option B SR model). No action needed unless you now disagree.
